title: CDSA-BB3: Pilot Biometric Pillar of the CDSA Federated Reinforcement Learning Paradigm — Synthetic Biometric Data, Anonymization, Cryptographic Multi-Signature, and Scaffolded FRL Extension
description:
CDSA-BB3 is the reference implementation of the pilot-biometrics pillar of the Complementary Diagnostic Safety Approach (CDSA), a third-generation aviation safety paradigm formalised as a Federated Reinforcement Learning (FRL) framework following the CDSA Methodological Unification Decision (Scenario C, 21 May 2026, frozen status).

The v1.0.0 release provides three Python modules: a deterministic generator producing 1179 synthetic biometric records, two-layer anonymization (k-Anonymity k=5 + Laplace differential privacy epsilon=1.0), and a cryptographic multi-signature scheme combining AES-256-GCM with Shamir 2/3 secret sharing.

The v2 scaffold (22 May 2026) adds five Python modules (rl_agents, rl_environment, federated, multimodal, xai) for the FRL paradigm extension. Full implementations are deferred to TÜBİTAK 1001 ARDEB Projects 1 + 2 (Sept 2026 application cycle).

creators.name: Cantekin, Mete
creators.affiliation: Department of Computer Engineering, Istanbul Beykent University

notes: v2 scaffold release (22 May 2026): paradigm-alignment update following CDSA Scenario C decision. Five new src/ modules are scaffolded for the FRL extension; full implementations are deferred to TUBITAK 1001 ARDEB Projects 1+2 (Sept 2026 cycle, 2027-2030 execution). The v1.0.0 release tag remains valid and reproducible — all cryptographic-biometric content unchanged.
references: Dwork C, Roth A. The algorithmic foundations of differential privacy. Foundations and Trends in Theoretical Computer Science; 2014.
Shamir A. How to share a secret. Communications of the ACM, 22(11):612-613; 1979.
National Institute of Standards and Technology. SP 800-38D: Recommendation for Block Cipher Modes of Operation: Galois/Counter Mode (GCM) and GMAC. NIST; 2007.
National Institute of Standards and Technology. SP 800-57 Part 1 Rev. 5: Recommendation for Key Management. NIST; 2020.
International Civil Aviation Organization. Annex 13 to the Convention on International Civil Aviation: Aircraft Accident and Incident Investigation. Twelfth Edition. ICAO; 2020.
International Civil Aviation Organization. Annex 19 to the Convention on International Civil Aviation: Safety Management. Second Edition. ICAO; 2016.
European Parliament and Council. Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation). Official Journal of the European Union; 2016.
Turkish Personal Data Protection Authority. Law No. 6698 on the Protection of Personal Data. Official Gazette No. 29677; 2016.
